sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: JetAI Adopts Poison Pill to Thwart Hostile Takeovers
feed.summary: Jet.AI has adopted a limited-duration stockholder rights agreement, effectively a poison pill, to deter hostile takeover attempts. The plan issues one preferred share purchase right per common share, which activates if any entity acquires 10% or more of the company's stock, allowing other shareholders to purchase shares at a discount. This measure aims to protect long-term shareholder value and expires in February 2027 unless redeemed earlier by the board.

feed.title: IPG Photonics Stock Hits 52-Week High After Strong Quarter
feed.summary: IPG Photonics (IPGP) stock soared after reporting strong fourth-quarter results that surpassed Wall Street expectations, pushing its shares to a new 52-week high. The company reported adjusted earnings of 46 cents per share and net sales of $274.47 million, driven by growth in materials processing, medical, and advanced applications. Despite a decline in adjusted gross margin due to higher costs, the company's CEO expressed optimism for continued revenue growth, citing strategic initiatives and rising demand in additive manufacturing and battery production.

feed.title: Realty Income (O) Valuation Check After GIC Partnership Notes Offering Strong Q3 And Dividend Growth
feed.summary: Realty Income (O) has seen increased investor interest following a partnership with Singapore's GIC, an $862.5 million convertible notes offering, strong Q3 results, and consistent monthly dividend increases. While one valuation model suggests it's slightly overvalued at $65.66 compared to a fair value of $64.31, another discounted cash flow model indicates a significant undervaluation, trading 34.7% below an estimated fair value of $100.61. The article encourages investors to consider their own assumptions on growth and margins when evaluating O.

feed.title: Is ConocoPhillips (COP) Pricing Reflect Its Strong Recent Run And Cash Flow Outlook
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (COP) is currently trading at US$111.43, and a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests it is significantly undervalued by 61.1%, with an estimated intrinsic value of $286.45 per share. While its P/E ratio of 17.09x is higher than the industry average, Simply Wall St's proprietary Fair Ratio of 24.70x indicates that the current P/E is still materially lower than its modeled reference level, further suggesting undervaluation. The article emphasizes that valuation narratives can vary, highlighting the importance of individual investor assumptions.

feed.title: Can Clorox (CLX) Sustain Its Transformation While Raising Dividends and Integrating Purell?
feed.summary: Clorox recently reported a slight dip in Q2 sales and net income but reaffirmed its full-year earnings guidance, maintaining its dividend streak and continuing a complex business transformation, including an ERP rollout and integrating the Purell acquisition. The company's investment narrative hinges on its ability to weather weak category growth and consumer trading down, with a focus on margin recovery as ERP disruption fades. Despite these efforts, analysts remain divided on its future prospects and fair value.

feed.title: Stryker’s New Humerus System Deepens Trauma Platform And Surgeon Ties
feed.summary: Stryker has launched the T2 Alpha Humerus Nailing System, a new trauma solution utilizing its SOMA technology to enhance efficiency and consistency in humerus fracture procedures. This strategic product release aims to standardize complex upper extremity trauma care for hospitals and surgeons, reinforcing Stryker's established presence in orthopedics. For investors, this move is seen as a way to deepen hospital relationships and surgeon loyalty, rather than a driver of short-term stock fluctuations.

feed.title: Werner Reports Higher Earnings, Revenues
feed.summary: Werner Enterprises Inc. reported significant increases in operating revenues and net income for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2002. The company's chairman attributed the improved performance to consistent freight demand, strategic fleet management, and a diversified freight base. Werner also noted industry-wide factors limiting truck capacity, contributing to a better operating margin.

feed.title: Bank of America stock price steadies near $52 after choppy week — what to watch next
feed.summary: Bank of America (BAC) stock closed Friday at $52.55, recovering slightly after a significant drop. The coming week is crucial for investors, with markets closed Monday, followed by the release of Federal Reserve meeting minutes and key inflation data. Additionally, news regarding CEO compensation, bank-capital regulations, and an ongoing lawsuit related to Jeffrey Epstein are factors influencing investor sentiment.

feed.title: FBRT Q4 Deep Dive: Management Resets Dividend and Emphasizes Shift to Recurring Revenue
feed.summary: Franklin BSP Realty Trust (NYSE:FBRT) reported Q4 CY2025 results that missed revenue and adjusted profit expectations, leading to a negative market reaction. Management attributed this to lower returns on new loan originations, tight lending spreads, and slow real estate owned (REO) asset liquidations, prompting a dividend reset and a strategic shift towards recurring servicing and fee income through its NewPoint acquisition. The company is focusing on stabilizing book value and building more consistent earnings, with future performance dependent on REO resolutions and interest rate movements.

feed.title: XPO (NYSE:XPO) Sheds 4.1% This Week, as Yearly Returns Fall More in Line With Earnings Growth
feed.summary: XPO, Inc. (NYSE:XPO) experienced a 4.1% share price drop this week, though its three-year return remains impressive at 424%. The company's earnings per share have grown 19% annually over three years, while its share price surged 74% per year, indicating increased market confidence. Insiders have also made significant purchases in the past year, suggesting positive internal outlook.

feed.title: GE Aerospace’s Singapore Bet Tests Growth Versus Rich Valuation
feed.summary: GE Aerospace is investing US$300 million to upgrade its Singapore repair hub with advanced technologies to increase capacity and reduce turnaround times for jet engine repairs. This investment aims to strengthen GE Aerospace's position in the growing Asia Pacific aviation market and address existing maintenance backlogs. For investors, the success of this project in shortening repair times and its impact on the company's valuation and capital allocation strategies will be crucial to monitor.

feed.title: Falling Short Interest Supports Positive Sentiment And Growth Outlook For TJX
feed.summary: Short interest in TJX Companies has recently declined, indicating reduced bearish sentiment and growing investor optimism. This aligns with positive analyst commentary and the company's strong performance as an off-price retailer. While structural risks remain, the low short interest compared to peers and positive earnings forecasts support a favorable outlook for TJX.

feed.title: Key facts: Exact Sciences to merge with Abbott by Q2 2026; 2025 revenue up 18%
feed.summary: Exact Sciences Corp. is set to merge with Abbott Laboratories by Q2 2026, a move contingent on regulatory approvals. The company also recently cleared a waiting period for its deal with Freenome Holdings. In 2025, Exact Sciences reported an 18% revenue increase, reaching $3.25 billion, with screening revenue specifically growing by 26% due to Cologuard tests and PreventionGenetics.

feed.title: Silvercorp Metals: Silver Miner Caught Between China Risk Jitters and Precious Metals Optimism
feed.summary: Silvercorp Metals (SVM) stock has been volatile, caught between investor concerns over its China exposure and optimism for precious metals. Despite a mixed short-term outlook and a 30% decline over the past year, analysts offer a cautious "Buy" rating, acknowledging the company's strong balance sheet and low operating costs. The future performance hinges on silver prices, sentiment towards Chinese assets, and the company's operational consistency.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Consumer Cyclical Companies: TripAdvisor (TRIP), DraftKings (DKNG) and Atmus Filtration Technologies, Inc. (ATMU)
feed.summary: The article highlights bullish sentiments from financial analysts regarding three consumer cyclical companies: TripAdvisor (TRIP), DraftKings (DKNG), and Atmus Filtration Technologies, Inc. (ATMU). Bernstein analysts maintained Buy ratings for TripAdvisor and DraftKings, while Northland Securities maintained a Buy rating for Atmus Filtration Technologies. The article details price targets, analyst ratings, success rates, and consensus opinions for each company.

feed.title: Prudential Versicherung Review: Is This Global Insurer the Safety Net Your Future Deserves?
feed.summary: This article reviews Prudential Versicherung (Prudential Insurance), focusing on its global offerings through Prudential plc, particularly in Asia and Africa. It highlights how Prudential aims to provide long-term financial security through a portfolio of life insurance, protection, and investment-linked solutions, emphasizing its global scale with local execution, long-term commitment, and investment expertise. The article also discusses user feedback, alternatives, and provides a final verdict for potential customers.

feed.title: AmpliTech Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMPG) Q2 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: This is an earnings call transcript for AmpliTech Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMPG) for their Q2 2025 results. The document contains a full record of the discussions held during the earnings call.

feed.title: The Truth About US Bancorp: Are You Sleeping on This Bank Stock Move?
feed.summary: This article examines US Bancorp (USB) as a potentially undervalued bank stock, contrasting its steady performance and dividend yield with the hype surrounding tech and crypto. It highlights the bank's diversified business model, reasonable valuation, and conservative management as reasons for long-term investors to consider it, despite its lack of "viral" appeal compared to other financial giants. The piece concludes that US Bancorp is a "grown-up money move" for stable, compounding returns rather than quick gains.

feed.title: Barclays Initiates Coverage of Microchip Technology (MCHP) with Equal-Weight Recommendation
feed.summary: Barclays has initiated coverage of Microchip Technology (MCHP) with an Equal-Weight recommendation. Analysts project an average one-year price target of $84.34, representing a 6.87% upside from its recent closing price. Institutional ownership shows a slight decrease in the last quarter, yet the put/call ratio indicates a bullish outlook for the company.

feed.title: Expedia Group Inc.: Can the Travel Super-App Keep Its Edge in an AI-First Booking World?
feed.summary: Expedia Group Inc. is transforming its travel marketplace into an AI-first, membership-driven ecosystem, aiming to offer a unified, intelligent platform for travelers through brands like Expedia, Hotels.com, and Vrbo. The company is leveraging its integrated tech stack, One Key loyalty program, and generative AI to compete with rivals like Booking Holdings, Airbnb, and Google Travel. Expedia Group faces challenges such as high customer acquisition costs but seeks to differentiate through its end-to-end ecosystem, B2B platform, genuine AI utility, and package deals, which are crucial for its stock's long-term profile.

feed.title: WisdomTree China ex-State-Owned Enterprises Fund (NASDAQ:CXSE) Sees Large Increase in Short Interest
feed.summary: Short interest in the WisdomTree China ex-State-Owned Enterprises Fund (CXSE) significantly increased by 52.6% in January, reaching 13,901 shares. Despite this surge, short positions remain small relative to the trading volume, with a days-to-cover ratio of just 0.1 days. Several institutional investors also either established new positions or increased their holdings in CXSE during the fourth quarter, indicating growing interest in the fund.

feed.title: The Real Reason Investors Should Be Excited for Ford's China Negotiations
feed.summary: Ford is facing challenges in its European operations, including slow EV adoption and competition from Chinese EV makers. The company plans a three-pronged strategy focusing on its Ford Pro commercial vehicle division, refreshing its passenger vehicle lineup, and improving cost efficiencies. A potential partnership with China's Geely, involving producing vehicles for Geely and sharing technology, could significantly benefit Ford by enabling faster and cheaper vehicle development, similar to Volkswagen's collaboration with Xpeng.

feed.title: What Might Be Behind One Fund's New $40 Million Bet on Rogers Corporation Stock?
feed.summary: ACK Asset Management LLC recently made a significant investment in Rogers Corporation (NYSE: ROG), acquiring over 436,000 shares valued at nearly $40 million. This new position represents 5% of the fund's assets under management and highlights Rogers' strategic importance in engineered materials for growing sectors like EVs and aerospace. The move suggests a belief in Rogers' long-term growth potential and margin durability, despite the company's stock already showing strong performance.

feed.title: Intuit (INTU) stock price near $400: AI scare trade, cooler CPI and the Feb. 26 earnings test
feed.summary: Intuit (INTU) stock closed at $399.40 on Friday, facing pressure from an "AI disruption" trade that has seen software stocks shed trillions. Despite cooler-than-expected CPI data providing some relief to growth stocks, concerns about AI's impact on subscription models have led to a significant 40% plunge in INTU shares this year. Investors are now keenly awaiting Intuit's Q2 earnings report on February 26 for updates on key products and management's stance on AI.

feed.title: Pinnacle Financial Awards Underscore Relationship Banking Strengths And Profitability Questions
feed.summary: Pinnacle Financial Partners (NYSE:PNFP) recently received a record number of Coalition Greenwich 2026 Best Bank Awards, highlighting its strength in relationship banking for small business and middle market clients. While these awards bolster brand perception and client retention, the bank faces challenges with a 3.2% net interest margin and a weaker efficiency ratio. The article suggests investors should monitor how Pinnacle translates its service reputation into profitable growth, especially as it integrates Synovus, and how it addresses profitability metrics.

feed.title: The Truth About US Bancorp: Are You Sleeping on This Bank Stock Move?
feed.summary: The article discusses US Bancorp (USB) as a potentially undervalued bank stock, often overlooked in favor of tech and crypto. It highlights USB's strong points like steady dividends, stability, and conservative management, making it appealing to long-term investors seeking consistent growth rather than quick, volatile gains. The piece encourages readers to consider US Bancorp for a diversified, stable portfolio, contrasting it with flashier but riskier investments.

feed.title: Wynn Resorts Advances UAE Flagship As International Mix And Valuation Diverge
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts announced the "topping out" of its Wynn Al Marjan Island tower in the UAE, signaling a significant step in its international expansion. The company expects over half of its future revenues to come from non-U.S. markets, aiming for a more geographically diverse business model. Despite this strategic move and ongoing property enhancements, the stock is trading about 70.5% above its estimated fair value, and interest payments are not well covered by earnings.

feed.title: 13 Best Innovative Stocks to Buy According to Wall Street Analysts
feed.summary: This article identifies 13 innovative stocks recommended by Wall Street analysts. It highlights Veracyte, Inc. (NASDAQ: VCYT) as an example, detailing UBS's reaffirmed Buy rating, a $48 price target, and positive commentary on its Q4 testing revenue, 2026 projections, and adjusted EBITDA margin. The company is a genomic diagnostics firm specializing in cancer and autoimmune disease tests.

feed.title: S&P Global stock rebounds into the weekend — analysts cut targets and SPGI’s next test looms
feed.summary: S&P Global (SPGI) stock rose 3.11% on Friday, despite Mizuho and BMO cutting their price targets while maintaining bullish ratings. The company recently reported strong Q4 2025 revenue growth of 9% and projected 2026 adjusted EPS between $19.40 and $19.65. Investors are now looking to forthcoming economic data, including the Federal Reserve's meeting minutes and PCE inflation data, as well as the company's monthly issuance updates, to gauge future performance.

feed.title: Intel AI Push With SambaNova Investment Meets Valuation And Supply Questions
feed.summary: Intel is reportedly considering a significant investment of up to US$100 million in AI startup SambaNova Systems to bolster its presence in artificial intelligence and high-end computing. This move, alongside the hiring of a new Chief GPU Architect, signals Intel's intensified focus on AI compute and GPUs, aiming to compete in areas like AI training and inference. However, persistent supply constraints and a current stock valuation significantly above estimated fair value present challenges to Intel's execution and market traction in these rapidly growing sectors.

feed.title: Hormel Taps Hershey Veteran To Tackle Supply Chain And Margin Pressures
feed.summary: Hormel Foods has appointed Will Bonifant, a Hershey veteran, as Chief Supply Chain Officer to address supply chain efficiency and input cost volatility. This strategic hire aims to tackle margin pressures and operational disruptions, leveraging Bonifant's extensive global experience in procurement, plant operations, logistics, and engineering. Investors will be watching for improvements in cost control, inventory management, and overall operational efficiency as a result of this leadership change.

feed.title: Assessing NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) Valuation After Q4 Beat And AI‑Focused Growth Guidance
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) has surpassed Q4 2025 market expectations and provided positive Q1 2026 guidance, driven by strong demand in automotive, industrial, and IoT sectors, with a particular focus on AI-related products. The stock is currently trading at a discount to analyst targets and intrinsic estimates, suggesting it is undervalued despite recent strong performance. The narrative anticipates continued growth due to the normalization of automotive inventory levels, leading to increased revenues and improved earnings visibility.

feed.title: Why Verizon (VZ) Is Up 5.8% After Major Consumer Leadership Shake-Up And Job Cuts - And What's Next
feed.summary: Verizon (VZ) saw its stock rise by 5.8% following significant leadership changes in its Consumer Group and the announcement of 13,000 job cuts, alongside plans to convert 179 corporate stores to franchises. These moves are part of CEO Dan Schulman's turnaround strategy, aiming to align consumer operations with the company's transformation agenda. The article discusses how these organizational shifts and the recent Frontier Communications acquisition could impact Verizon's investment narrative, emphasizing the need for successful execution to translate network strengths and cost cuts into stable revenue and cash generation.

feed.title: Efficiency Drive Ahead of Syra Health's Q4 2025 Results
feed.summary: Syra Health is focusing on digital health and behavioral medicine to strengthen its operating platform and reduce losses ahead of its Q4 2025 results. Investors will be observing the March earnings report closely for signs of effective cost-control and progress towards profitability. The company aims to stand out in the evolving health technology sector by integrating its services into modern care networks.

feed.title: Caesars Casino Bonus Code ROTOCASLAUNCH in Feb: Score $1,010 & 2.5K Rewards Credits
feed.summary: The article details the Caesars Casino bonus code "ROTOCASLAUNCH" for February 2026, offering new players a $10 no-deposit bonus, a 100% deposit match up to $1,000, and 2,500 Caesars Rewards Credits. This promotion is available in Michigan, New Jersey, West Virginia, and Pennsylvania, providing value for both casual and high-volume bettors with specific wagering requirements and integration into the Caesars Rewards program.

feed.title: TravelCenters of America Opens Nominations for Citizen Driver Award
feed.summary: TravelCenters of America (TA) is currently accepting nominations for its 2018 Citizen Driver award, which recognizes truck drivers for their good citizenship, safety practices, community involvement, health and wellness, and leadership. Nominations are open to anyone and will be accepted until September 20th. Winners will be honored at a ceremony on May 8, 2018, at the TA-branded Iowa 80 location.

feed.title: 3 Dividend Stocks I Love to Buy for Passive Income
feed.summary: The author is passionate about generating passive income and highlights three dividend stocks they favor: Brookfield Infrastructure, Enterprise Products Partners, and Realty Income. These companies are chosen for their stable cash flows, consistent dividend increases, and strong growth prospects, which help the author move closer to financial independence. Each company has a history of steadily increasing their payouts, making them attractive for long-term passive income generation.

feed.title: Principal Financial Group® 2023 Sustainability Report: A Letter From Our CEO
feed.summary: Principal Financial Group's CEO, Dan Houston, highlights the company's commitment to financial security, integrity, and sustainability in their 2023 Sustainability Report. The report focuses on achievements in financial inclusion, workplace diversity, environmental goals, and community investment through the Principal Foundation. Houston emphasizes the dedication of employees and the company's continuous efforts to meet customer expectations and progress in ESG initiatives.

feed.title: Ethereum Bets Return: Invesco’s QETH ETF Sees Big Inflow Despite Price Slump
feed.summary: The Invesco Galaxy Ethereum ETF (QETH) experienced a significant inflow of $2.23 million on February 6, 2026, representing about 9.3% of its total assets under management, even as Ethereum (ETH-USD) prices have seen a 38.6% slide over three months. This suggests that some investors are anticipating a rebound in Ethereum and are using the ETF to either average down their exposure or capitalize on future catalysts. Despite short-term technical indicators flashing "Sell" for ETH-USD, the substantial ETF inflows highlight a divergence in market sentiment.

feed.title: Assessing Turning Point Brands (TPB) Valuation After Oppenheimer’s Bullish Nicotine Pouch Outlook
feed.summary: Turning Point Brands (TPB) is gaining investor attention after a bullish update from Oppenheimer, driven by strong anticipated Q4 2025 performance and its position in the growing nicotine pouch market. Despite recent share price gains making TPB appear overvalued compared to its $121.25 fair value, its ability to grow premium brands and maintain strong margins supports cash generation, though regulatory pressures remain a key risk. Investors are encouraged to assess the company's fundamentals and consider other opportunities.

feed.title: Olema Pharmaceuticals CEO Teases Fall OPERA-01 Readout, Q2 KAT6 Data and CFO/COO Exit
feed.summary: Olema Pharmaceuticals CEO Sean Bohen provided updates on the company's pipeline, including an expected Fall readout for the OPERA-01 trial of palazestrant and Q2 2026 data for the KAT6 inhibitor OP-3136. Bohen also addressed the departure of CFO/COO Shane Kovacs, framing it as the company prepares for potential commercialization of palazestrant in the U.S. and seeks ex-U.S. partners. He emphasized palazestrant's potential as a best-in-class oral SERD, citing its pharmacologic attributes and past clinical results.

feed.title: QVC Is Facing Bankruptcy, in Talks to Restructure Debt: Report
feed.summary: QVC Group Inc., the company behind home-shopping channels QVC and HSN, is reportedly considering filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy due to a substantial debt burden. The company and its lenders are in confidential negotiations for a voluntary debt restructuring agreement. As of September 30, QVC Group had $6.6 billion in outstanding debt and also faces a tax liability.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses GTA & Take-Two (TTWO) Interactive
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed Take-Two Interactive Software Inc. (TTWO) on Mad Money, calling it a great stock despite its recent struggles. Wells Fargo and BMO Capital have both raised their price targets for TTWO, citing strong bookings and operational performance. Cramer also highlighted that the Grand Theft Auto series could boost the company's stock significantly.

feed.title: Proctor (PG)'s a Machine Despite Slow Growth, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer describes Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) as a "machine" despite its slow organic growth. The company's shares are down 6% over the past year but up 13% year-to-date. Analysts have issued mixed ratings, with some noting sluggish organic sales growth but potential for fundamental improvement.

feed.title: Colgate (CL) Had Great Growth, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer praised Colgate-Palmolive Company's recent earnings report, highlighting its better-than-expected quarter and organic growth. The company's shares have performed well, with Barclays and Jefferies raising price targets, though Barclays noted potential challenges in maintaining past price growth momentum in emerging markets. While acknowledging Colgate's potential, the article suggests that some AI stocks may offer higher returns with limited downside risk.

feed.title: Needham noted value-accretive acquisition activity for SS&C Technologies Holdings, Inc. (SSNC)
feed.summary: Needham highlighted SS&C Technologies Holdings, Inc. (SSNC) for its value-accretive acquisition strategy. The company is actively pursuing acquisitions that contribute positively to its financial performance. This strategy is seen as a key factor in Needham's assessment of SS&C.

feed.title: UGI Corp: Utility Value Play Or Value Trap As The Stock Treads Water?
feed.summary: UGI Corp's stock has been trading in a tight range recently, but over the past year, it has seen a double-digit slide, indicating a challenging period for the company. Analysts are cautiously optimistic with a "Hold" rating, suggesting limited near-term upside. The company's future performance hinges on effective infrastructure investment, maintaining LPG margins, and prudent capital allocation amidst evolving energy markets and regulatory frameworks.

feed.title: 10 Best Medical Technology Stocks to Invest In
feed.summary: This article identifies STERIS plc (NYSE:STE) as one of the best medical technology stocks to invest in, highlighting its strong financial performance. The company exceeded revenue expectations and matched profit forecasts for Q3 FY2026, demonstrating robust growth driven by demand across multiple business lines. Analysts maintain a "Market Outperform" rating with a $280 price target, recognizing STERIS's strong position in the medical technology sector.

feed.title: Republic Services Inc.'s New Fleet Of Natural Gas Powered Trucks Arrives In Houston
feed.summary: Republic Services Inc. has deployed an additional 49 compressed natural gas (CNG) solid waste and recycling trucks in Houston, bringing their total new CNG trucks in the area to 104 within the past year. These trucks replace older diesel-powered vehicles and are supported by two new natural gas fueling stations. The company highlights the environmental benefits, stating that each new CNG truck significantly reduces ozone-forming emissions and is equivalent to removing five passenger vehicles from local roads annually.

feed.title: Quantum computing stocks face a holiday week after IonQ stake filing and a Rigetti downgrade
feed.summary: Quantum computing stocks are facing headwinds with a holiday-shortened week, driven by a disclosure that Morgan Stanley's stake in IonQ has dropped below 5% and TD Cowen's downgrade of Rigetti to "hold" due to valuation and funding risks. Traders are anticipating late-February earnings reports from D-Wave and IonQ, where guidance is expected to significantly influence market direction. The sector remains sensitive to cash flow and customer projections, with potential for renewed dilution worries if growth expectations falter.
